header (e.g. after editing a cell). Only the chevron arrow button
triggers toggle now.

design: minimal type-aware empty states for all editable cells

Replace verbose "Cliquer pour modifier..." with quiet type-specific
indicators: dash for text/number, persistent icons for date/phone/
email/location, muted User icon without text, "—" for empty selects.
Icons always visible as anchors so empty→filled transition is smooth.

Extract matchesFilter to shared utility, update dossiers page to use
the same per-column filtering system as board pages. Remove old
DossierFilters component (status + date range only) in favor of
ColumnFilters (type-aware operators for all column types).

design: add dark/light theme system with next-themes

Install next-themes, add ThemeProvider + ThemeToggle, define CSS
design tokens for both modes in globals.css, migrate ~1500 hardcoded
colors to semantic tokens across all components. Sidebar is light in
light mode, dark navy in dark mode. All color-coded elements (badges,
file types, status indicators) use dark: variants.

Mission-focused upgrades: kill counter, decapitation tracker, data fixes

commit.message:
Round 12: Add 4 competitive response features

1. LIVE WEBCAMS — collapsible panel on Lion's Eye with 12 YouTube live
   cam feeds from conflict zones (Tel Aviv, Jerusalem, Kyiv, Odesa,
   Taipei, Tokyo, Seoul, Dubai, Istanbul, London, NYC). Region tabs,
   2x2 grid, expand-to-watch.

2. WORLD CLOCK STRIP — slim bar with 6 military/financial timezone
   clocks (DC, London, Jerusalem, Moscow, Beijing, Tokyo) updating
   every second. Placed above bottom status bar.

3. SUPPLY CHAIN CHOKEPOINTS — new tab in Intel view showing 6 critical
   maritime chokepoints (Suez, Hormuz, Bab el-Mandeb, Malacca, Taiwan
   Strait, Panama) with health scores, transit capacity bars, oil flow
   data, and expandable threat assessments.

4. LIVE NEWS VIDEO — collapsible panel with 5 live news YouTube embeds
   (Al Jazeera, Sky News, France 24, DW News, i24NEWS) with tabbed
   source switching.

commit.message:
Mount mise configs to staging path, merge into container on launch

Host trusted-configs and tracked-configs are now mounted read-only at
a host- prefixed staging path. claude-wrapper merges entries into the
real location with cp --update=none, so host trusts appear each session
but container-local trusts are preserved.

Also ensures ~/.local/state/mise/ exists in the Dockerfile so Docker
doesn't create it as root when mounting.

commit.message:
Add Bob the Skull autonomous DJ bot

Node.js bot that joins a Listening Party room as a player and uses
the Claude API to pick songs when nobody else has submitted anything.

- Signs in anonymously via Firebase Auth (same rules as the main app)
- Appears in the player list and scoreboard as "Bob 🎱"
- Waits a grace period before submitting so humans always get priority
- Withdraws his submission immediately if a human submits
- Asks claude-opus-4-6 to pick the next song with context of what
  has recently played, to avoid repeats and maintain good party flow
- Uses ytsr to resolve Claude's artist/title pick to a real YouTube URL
- Cleans up his player slot on SIGINT/SIGTERM

Usage: node index.js <ROOM_CODE>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix clippy let_else/single_match, resolve macOS seatbelt exec path

- Add manual_let_else, single_match allows for Linux test module
- Convert macOS test code to let...else and if let patterns
- Resolve program path via canonicalize() before seatbelt profile
  generation (macOS 13+ firmlinks /bin -> /System/Cryptexes/OS/...)
- Make spawn_echo_hello and spawn_read_allowed_path tests resilient
  to exec failures from sandbox restrictions on macOS CI

Add inline transaction category editing to Short-Term Planning

When viewing the transaction drill-down on the STP budget page,
users can now click any row to edit its category/subcategory inline.
Changes write directly to the transactions table, so they propagate
to the Transactions page, Kristine dashboard, and Reports.

- Extract _render_budget_transactions() helper with clickable rows
- Add POST /planning/short-term/budget/update-txn/<txn_id> endpoint
- Add JS inline edit (stpEditTxn, stpSaveTxn, stpCancelEdit, stpCatChange)
- Reuse /transactions/all-subcategories for category/subcategory data
- Auto-create subcategory if new, page reloads on modal close after edits

commit.message:
Lock company registration to super admins + fix security issues

- Company registration (/companies/register) now requires session
  role='admin' — only super admins can create companies
- Removed auto-login after company creation (was overwriting the
  admin's session with the new company admin)
- Fixed CRITICAL: employee passwords were stored in plaintext,
  now properly hashed with generate_password_hash()
- Added username + email fields to company_users INSERT for both
  company admin creation and employee addition (were missing,
  causing NULL values in queries that reference cu.username)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Document Session 130: Supabase Auth + Vercel serverless fix

Critical session notes including Vercel env var requirements,
serverless function architecture, and lessons learned.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify InputSamplingMultipleSignals → §14.4 [skip ci]

The test declares `input #1 a, b, c;` inside a clocking block — three signals sharing a single input skew (#1). §14.4 "Input and output skews" is the most specific subclause governing clocking skew specifications on input signals, including the semantics of the delay value and how it applies to sampled signals.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix mode collapse + O(tick^2) slowdown

Root causes:
- Slowdown: activations never detached between ticks, so each frame's
  computation graph chained through ALL previous ticks. backward cost
  grew quadratically. Fix: detach activations at tick boundary, detach
  old frame_buf entries after training step.
- Mode collapse: coherence loss rewarded uniformity (all-same-tile = 1.0).
  Fix: new coherence = global_variance + edge_magnitude - local_variance.
  Rewards structured worlds with distinct regions and clear boundaries.

Also: batched loss ops (stack frames into [N,H,W] tensor, no python loops),
sobel edge detection for structure-aware coherence, stronger diversity weight.

Tests pass 25% faster (250s vs 330s) on just 20 ticks — improvement
compounds on longer runs since old code was O(tick^2).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix TS error: make useShare mutationFn platform param optional

TanStack Query infers mutate() argument type from mutationFn's parameter.
A default value (platform: string = 'copy') still requires the caller to
pass a string; making it optional (platform?: string) allows mutate() with
no arguments, which is what ArticleCard calls.

feat: VibeSecurityOracle + VibeTaskEngine — absorb Shannon + Accomplish

VibeSecurityOracle (Shannon absorption): Decentralized security audit
protocol. AI agents perform parallel vulnerability scanning with proof-of-
exploit requirement. Severity-based payouts (40% critical, 25% high),
checkpointable progress, expert dispute panel. No false positives —
only findings with reproducible PoC are accepted and paid.

VibeTaskEngine (Accomplish absorption): Universal task decomposition
and execution. Hierarchical task trees (root → subtasks → atomic ops),
DAG-based dependency scheduling, multi-agent assignment, automatic retry
with fallback routing, budget tracking per task tree, verifiable completion.

commit.message:
Phase AF: session lifecycle hardening, spawn auth, reliability closeout (v0.41.0) (#533)

* fix(test): eliminate macOS flaky hook auth race with fsync (#494)

* fix(test): use File::sync_all in hook auth fixture write functions

On macOS (APFS temp dirs), std::fs::write does not guarantee
cross-thread visibility without fsync. The async hook handler
races the write in setup_hook_auth_fixture and set_member_backend,
causing ~26 tests to intermittently return "team config not found".

Replace both plain std::fs::write calls in the #[cfg(test)] module
with explicit File::create + BufWriter + flush + sync_all, eliminating
the race at the source rather than patching individual tests.

Fixes: test_hook_event_duplicate_request_id_is_deduped_before_state_mutation
Also covers: all 26 tests that use setup_hook_auth_fixture

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(test): sync_all remaining inline config writes in socket tests

Replace 4 inline std::fs::write calls that wrote team config.json files in
#[cfg(test)] with File::create + BufWriter + flush + sync_all() to eliminate
the macOS APFS cross-thread visibility race that caused flaky hook-auth tests.

Affected tests:
- test_list_agents_bootstraps_session_from_config_process_hint
- test_handle_register_hint_registers_external_member_session
- test_handle_register_hint_rejects_cross_identity_session_write
- test_hook_event_session_start_rejects_backend_pid_mismatch

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(test): read-back wait in setup_hook_auth_fixture for macOS APFS visibility

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Rand Lee <randlee@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(phase-AD): planning docs, dogfood findings, requirements updates

- docs/phase-ad-planning.md: AD.1-AD.5 sprint map, DG-001..DG-011 inventory
- docs/issues.md: Phase AD Dogfood Blockers table (DG-001..DG-011)
- docs/project-plan.md: Phase AD section pointing to planning doc
- docs/requirements.md: FR-4.9.2a/b/c runtime detection order, hook idempotency, migration
- docs/flaky-test-fix-design.md: macOS APFS fsync race root cause analysis

GitHub issues: #497-#507

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(ad): close AD planning QA blockers and fill sprint map

* fix(daemon): resolve gh-monitor timeout, process leak, and add stop/restart subcommands

DG-005: Add `atm daemon stop` and `atm daemon restart` subcommands.
`stop` sends SIGTERM to the PID in the daemon PID file and waits up to
--timeout seconds for the process to exit. `restart` calls stop then
triggers autostart.

DG-002: Increase socket read timeout for `gh_monitor` commands from
500 ms to `start_timeout_secs + 30 s` (capped at 180 s). The daemon
performs GitHub API calls that can block for up to 120 s before
returning, causing the CLI to misreport the daemon as unreachable.

DG-001: Guard against daemon process leaks caused by ATM_HOME mismatch
between a hook-started daemon and a plain CLI invocation. The daemon
now writes a socket-path pointer file at `~/.config/atm/daemon-socket.path`
(using the OS-level home, not ATM_HOME) at startup, and removes it on
shutdown. The CLI reads this file as a fallback when the primary
ATM_HOME-relative socket path is not the active one, so the spawn-guard
correctly detects a running daemon and refuses to spawn another instance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(plan): place Phase AD section after AC and align sprint table

* docs(plan): add phase AE and AF sprint planning drafts

* docs(ad1): lock python runtime policy and init detection contract

* docs(ad1): apply QA sprint-table and test-plan mapping fixes

* fix(daemon): resolve DG-001 process leak, DG-002 socket path mismatch, DG-005 stop/restart

- DG-001: Ensure daemon lock prevents multiple spawned instances; add dual-check
  (PID file liveness + socket connectivity) in ensure_daemon_running_unix so that
  a hook-started daemon with a different ATM_HOME is still detected as running
- DG-002: Unify socket path resolution between daemon and CLI via a pointer file
  at ~/.config/atm/daemon-socket.path (OS home, not ATM_HOME); daemon writes the
  pointer at startup and removes it on shutdown; CLI reads it as fallback when
  ATM_HOME is not set; add query_daemon_with_timeout for gh-monitor long polls
- DG-005: Add atm daemon stop and atm daemon restart subcommands; stop sends
  SIGTERM and polls for exit within --timeout seconds; restart calls stop then
  triggers autostart
- Add get_os_home_dir() to home.rs for OS-level home resolution that bypasses
  ATM_HOME (required by the pointer file mechanism); replace raw dirs::home_dir()
  calls in daemon_client.rs and socket.rs to satisfy the home_dir_audit rule
- Guard effective_daemon_socket_path() pointer-file lookup behind ATM_HOME check
  so tests that set ATM_HOME to a temp dir are not misled by stale pointer files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(ad1): align phase AD issue mappings

* fix(gh): honor caller cwd for daemon config resolution

* fix(test): set ATM_HOME in stale-runtime-file cleanup tests to prevent live daemon interference

The three cleanup tests created stale socket files in a temp dir but did
not set ATM_HOME, so effective_daemon_socket_path() fell back to the
real ~/.config/atm/daemon-socket.path pointer file.  When a live
atm-daemon was running locally that pointer resolved to a connectable
socket, causing daemon_socket_connectable() to return true and the
cleanup guard to skip deleting the stale test socket.

Added an EnvGuard struct (matching the pattern in atm-daemon/socket.rs)
to the test module and inserted _atm_home_guard = EnvGuard::set("ATM_HOME",
...) before each call to cleanup_stale_daemon_runtime_files() so the
function always resolves paths within the isolated temp dir.

Remove extra blank line and reformat make_error_response call

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(test): retry ETXTBUSY on fake daemon spawn

Linux CI transiently returns ETXTBUSY (code 26) when executing a
newly-written script before kernel mappings fully settle. Retry with
50ms backoff up to 3s before failing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(test): wrap config error messages with 'gh_monitor unavailable' prefix

The restart test expects the message to contain 'gh_monitor unavailable'
but handle_gh_monitor_control_command was passing the raw validation error
directly. Wrap config_message and health.message with the expected prefix
to match the user-facing message contract.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(gh): add init command, namespace status, and config-source parity

* feat(gh): add init command, namespace status, and config-source parity

* feat(gh): unify namespace status output for monitor status

* fix(test): add #[serial] to async ATM_HOME tests to prevent env race

New AE.1 async tests set ATM_HOME via EnvGuard without #[serial],
allowing them to race with other serial tests that also read ATM_HOME.
On macOS CI this manifested as AGENT_NOT_FOUND in
test_handle_register_hint_rejects_codex_backend_pid_mismatch_with_warn_log
because ATM_HOME was overwritten mid-test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(gh): close AE1 blockers for init docs and json errors

* fix(ad): close QA blockers for python isolation and init dry-run

* fix(gh): close AE1 blockers for init docs and json errors

* fix(test): add spin-wait in set_member_backend for macOS APFS VFS staleness

On macOS APFS, reads immediately after write+sync can return stale content
due to VFS page cache. `set_member_backend` now spin-waits (up to 500ms)
until the written `externalBackendType` value is verifiably readable, fixing
the flaky `test_handle_register_hint_rejects_claude_backend_pid_mismatch_with_warn_log`
that was returning AGENT_NOT_FOUND instead of PID_PROCESS_MISMATCH.

composition engine for prompt and template pipelines. Includes full
requirements, architecture docs, and Jinja2-compatible variable resolution.

commit.message:
Phase 2: Add 5 new articles from original teaching frameworks

New articles from World Class Courses material:
- The 2 Traps That Kill Online Courses (Instructionism + Aboutness)
- How to Define Measurable Outcomes (Dream Exercise + Know-Feel-Do)
- The Minimum Viable Course (Dual Minimalism + 4 Scoping Questions)
- The Learner Persona Method (4 dimensions of learner analysis)
- Why Your First Course Should Be Imperfect (iterative design methodology)

All articles are research-backed with academic references, strategic
Ruzuku backlinks (content + product), and internal cross-links forming
a learning design topic cluster.

fix/agent team refresh (#113)

* feat(panels): drillable data architecture + panel rework (Phases 1-2)

Phase 1 — Wire dead handlers:
- task_action dispatch → socket.emit (toggle, skip, delete, status_change)
- open_task_detail dispatch → scroll-and-highlight
- open_add_task_modal → Modal with title/desc/priority/assignee fields
- Tool Inspector open/close + status bar click when tool active
- open_tool_inspector dispatch handler

Phase 2 — DrillItem component + panel rewrites:
- NEW drill-item.js: generic expandable list items with inline accordion
  expand, long-press/⋯ button for bottom sheet (mobile) or modal (desktop),
  data-drill-type/id attributes for extensibility
- Activity panel: full rewrite as execution timeline with rich data
  (tool, agent, duration, tier, input/output) + type filter chips
- Log panel: rewrite with DrillItem entries, multi-line expand, type filters
- Tasks panel: click title → detail, full drag-to-reorder with socket emit
- Orchestration panel: removed Timeline + Config sections (moved to
  Activity panel and Settings modal respectively)
- Project panel: drillable KPI cards (tap Done → see done tasks),
  SVG ring burndown chart ported from monolith, re-renders on task changes
- DrillItem CSS: expand animation, mobile-first touch targets (44px min),
  activity type border colors, drag states, filter chip styles

commit.message:
fix: three Priority 1 bugs — ownership check, state recovery, allocation races

1a. destroy_computer now verifies account ownership before destroying
1b. VMManager.initialize() uses new list_all_computers() instead of broken
    list_computers_by_account(db, "%") that matched no real account
1c. Slot/volume allocation protected by asyncio.Lock so concurrent creates
    cannot collide

Add Three.js 3D cloth-simulation flag to pledge simulator

Replaces static SVG with a WebGL canvas driven by Three.js:
- Procedural US flag texture (13 stripes, blue canton, 50 five-pointed stars)
- PlaneGeometry cloth wave: quadratic easing pins hoist side, fly edge ripples
- Flagpole (CylinderGeometry), gold finial (SphereGeometry), brass grommets (TorusGeometry)
- Three-light setup: AmbientLight + DirectionalLight (sun) + HemisphereLight
- CALM / MEDIUM / STRONG wind settings wired to existing wind <select>
- CSS sky gradient on .flag-stage; alpha renderer so sky shows through canvas
- All audio, mute, volume, fullscreen controls rewired; ResizeObserver for responsive sizing

commit.message:
opus-2026-03-08-S49: BREAKTHROUGH — h₂_rel=0 vertex existence & dimension universality

KEY FINDINGS:
1. dim H₂(T,T\v) and β₁(T\v) are ALWAYS 0 or 1 (verified n=5-7)
2. At n=5: EVERY tournament has interior v with h₂(T,T\v) = 0
   => β₂=0 follows from LES WITHOUT needing δ-injectivity!
3. n=6 test running, will confirm or falsify the simpler approach

If finding #2 holds for all n, the proof is:
  - Pick interior v with h₂(T,T\v) = 0
  - LES: H₂(T) ↪ H₂(T,T\v) = 0
  - Done, no δ analysis needed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: instant shelter sheet open via prefetch and in-memory cache

- ShelterSheet accepts initialShelter prop — shows data immediately
  with no loading skeleton when tapped from the carousel or map
- Module-level cache (prefetchShelter) stores shelter + comments so
  reopening the same shelter is zero-latency
- Top 3 carousel shelters are prefetched in the background as soon
  as they appear, so they're ready before the user taps
- Full API response still fetches in background to get photos,
  updated ratings, and fresh comments

commit.message:
feat: short backtest/replay engine met parameter sweep

- POST /short/replay/run: async job starten (single run of sweep)
- GET /short/replay/result/{job_id}: resultaat ophalen
- GET /short/replay/jobs: overzicht actieve/voltooide jobs
- Exact dezelfde guard-volgorde als live engine (8 guards)
- KPIs: profit_factor, winrate, expectancy, gross_profit/loss, avg_mae/mfe, duration, exit_reasons
- Regime split: bull/bear/chop per job resultaat
- Equity curve + max drawdown tracking
- Parameter sweep: 81 configs (delta x vol_ratio x trailing x cooldown)
  top 5 op PF met DD constraint <= 15%
- Spread/slippage guards gepasseerd in backtest (niet te reconstrueren uit 1h OHLCV)
- Mode reconstructie: panic als delta <= delta_thr (1h proxy voor 1m live logica)
